questions.question: By how much does adding LinkedIn to email-only outreach typically increase reply rates?
questions.explanation: Going from email-only to email + LinkedIn + voice typically increases reply rates by 25-35%. However, it also increases complexity by 3x and costs 4-5x more ($170/month vs $37/month). The question is whether the extra replies justify the additional cost and management time.

questions.question: Which multi-channel configuration does the lesson recommend for most solo founders?

options.text: Configuration A: Lemlist Multichannel Expert — all-in-one simplicity beats separated tools
Configuration B: Instantly + HeyReach — best for LinkedIn-heavy strategies
Configuration C: La Growth Machine — best for European markets
All three configurations are equally good

questions.explanation: Configuration A (Lemlist Multichannel Expert at $163/month) wins for most solo founders because all-in-one simplicity beats separated tools. Configuration B is recommended only if you're LinkedIn-heavy, and Configuration C if you're in Europe or need Twitter integration.

questions.question: When should you upgrade from email-only to multi-channel outreach?

options.text: After 6 weeks of consistent email results (8%+ reply rate, 3+ meetings/week) when you need incremental pipeline growth
Only when you have a dedicated SDR team

questions.explanation: Upgrade to multi-channel after proving email works (6 weeks of consistent results with 8%+ reply rates and 3+ meetings/week). Adding channels before validating email creates complexity without a proven foundation. Multi-channel adds 25-35% more replies but requires 30-40% more management time.

questions.question: The multi-channel stack requires approximately 30-40% more time to manage sequences across platforms compared to email-only.
questions.explanation: True. Multi-channel outreach increases management complexity by approximately 30-40% because you need to coordinate sequences across email and LinkedIn, manage multiple platform dashboards, and ensure channel-appropriate messaging. The 25-35% reply rate improvement must justify this additional time investment.

questions.question: Is your outreach currently at the point where multi-channel makes sense? Evaluate your email-only performance (reply rates, meetings booked, consistency) and make the case for or against upgrading to multi-channel right now.
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the response honestly assesses their current email performance with specific metrics, applies the readiness criteria from the lesson (6 weeks consistent, 8%+ reply rate, 3+ meetings/week), and reaches a justified conclusion. Look for understanding that multi-channel adds complexity and should only be adopted when email is already working.
